hero.headline: Most people will spend their whole life wondering if God had more for them. You don't have to.
hero.sub: One year. Everything changes. A family of 21 campuses across 4 countries already waiting for you.

subVariants.A: Three ways in. A family of 21 campuses across 4 countries already waiting for you.
subVariants.B: The year your child turns eighteen happens once. Help them spend it on something that compounds.
subVariants.C: You've built a career. Now build something that outlives it.

hook.headline: Three free sessions — no email required.
hook.sub: These are real lectures from the college. Not a trailer. The actual thing. Pick the one that speaks to where you are.

sessions.title: What's Wrong with the Church
sessions.forPersona: School leavers · Spiritually restless · Distance learners
sessions.blurb: A direct, honest diagnosis of where the Western Church lost its way — and what comes next. This is the lecture that makes people call their friends.

sessions.title: The Grace & Favor Revolution
sessions.blurb: If you've ever felt like church was more about performance than presence, this is for you. Ps Jane Evans on the revolution that changes everything.

sessions.title: Multiply or Die
sessions.blurb: For people who feel the pull toward something larger than themselves. The theology of multiplication — why the Church that doesn't reproduce, doesn't survive.

whyNow.headline: The Church must lead the change — not react to it.
whyNow.body: A generation is searching for something real. This is not a moment to shrink back. It is a moment to outthink, outbuild, and outlead — with theology that holds and skill that ships.

streams.title: Audit
streams.pitch: Come just to learn. Sit under every lecture, every subject. No exams.
streams.for: The curious believer. The working professional. The parent back for a second chapter.

streams.title: Academic
streams.pitch: For the degree. Alphacrucis-accredited. The long walk.
streams.for: The student who wants the paper, the rigour, and the lifelong qualification.

streams.title: Internship
streams.pitch: Come live it. Hands-on placement alongside a senior pastor. The year that changes everything.
streams.for: The called leader. Already sure. Wants to be sharpened, sent, and surrounded.

streams.title: One subject
streams.tagline: The easiest yes on the page.
streams.pitch: Can't commit to a full year? Start with one subject. Finish it. Then decide what's next.
streams.for: For anyone who needs a low-commitment on-ramp to find out what Futures College is really like.

programme.headline: Eight subjects. Three electives. One sending.

core.title: The Grace & Favor Revolution
core.tagline: The gospel as power, not performance.

core.title: Multiply or Die
core.tagline: Why every campus must plant a campus.

core.title: New Testament Survey & Birth of the Church
core.tagline: Walk the first century like an insider.

core.title: Applied Theology
core.tagline: From the text to the team meeting.

core.title: AI for Leaders: Multiply Your Impact Daily
core.tagline: Tools that give you hours back, every week.

core.title: Choose Your Elective
core.tagline: One deep dive. Pick the track that fits your calling.

electives.title: Kids Ministry
electives.blurb: Discipleship that starts early and sticks.

electives.title: Youth Ministry
electives.blurb: A generation worth fighting for, led well.

electives.title: Worship
electives.blurb: Songs, sound, stage — a room that meets Jesus.

outcomes: Lead a team or ministry by graduation.
Publish and preach a full series.
Ship a real project with a real P&L or portfolio.
programme.yearTwoNote: Year Two is available for deeper study and specialisation.

faculty.name: Ashley Evans
faculty.bio: Global Senior Pastor of Futures Church. 26 years of pastoral leadership across 4 countries.

faculty.name: Jane Evans
faculty.bio: Founder of bU Women. 20 years mentoring emerging female leaders across the globe.

experience.label: Morning prayer + liturgy at the chapel
Practicum — embedded in a Futures campus

alumni.name: Jordan Mitchell
alumni.quote: I came in thinking I’d take a gap year. I left knowing exactly who I was called to be.

alumni.name: Rachel Torres
alumni.quote: The teaching was honest. The practicum was real. I walked in a student and walked out a leader.

alumni.name: Marcus Williams
alumni.quote: My mentors still pick up the phone. That’s the difference.

alumni.name: Emma Chen
alumni.quote: They treated Jesus seriously and enterprise seriously.

notes: Need-based scholarships cover up to 60% of tuition for qualifying students.
Merit-based scholarships available for top applicants (up to 40%).
Employer / campus-sponsored pathway: your sending church can cover 25–100%.
Interest-free payment plans up to 24 months.

enrollment.headline: Starts September 2026.
enrollment.sub: Early-bird pricing closes May 29. Enrollment closes July 15.

online.headline: Futures Online — the full programme, self-paced.

closing: You cannot build what you cannot see. You cannot lead what you don't understand. You cannot plant what hasn't first taken root in you. One year changes that. The future belongs to those who act first.

faq.a: One year for the core programme. Year Two is optional for students pursuing deeper study or specialisation.
The 2026 cohort begins September 2026. Early-bird enrollment closes May 29, 2026. Final enrollment closes July 15, 2026.
Yes — through our partnership with Alphacrucis College, the Academic stream carries degree-level credit.

faq.q: What’s the difference between the streams?
faq.a: Audit (~96 hrs) is lecture-only, no assessment. Academic (~192 hrs) is accredited, with full assessment. Internship (~480 hrs) adds a hands-on placement under a senior pastor.
Atlanta (USA) and Adelaide (Australia), with online cohorts serving students worldwide.
Audit: ~2 hrs/week. Academic: ~4 hrs/week. Internship: ~10 hrs/week including on-campus placement.
Because the leaders who learn to use AI well will get hours back every week — and lead further, faster. Subject 07 makes it practical.
A high-school diploma (or equivalent) and a posture of teachability. We interview every applicant.
Year One is $14,800 USD. Need-based and merit-based scholarships cover up to 60%. Typical out-of-pocket after aid is ~$6,800/year.

faq.q: Can I take just one subject?
faq.a: Yes. The single-subject pathway is designed exactly for this. You enrol in one subject, finish it, and then decide if you want to continue. There's no pressure to commit further. It's the easiest yes on the page — and for many people, it becomes the first step of a full year.

faq.q: Is there a discount for early applicants?
faq.a: Yes — Founder's Circle applicants who apply before 29 May 2026 with code FOUNDER500 receive $500 off their first year. This is limited to the first 100 applicants. Once those spots are filled, the code expires.

faq.q: Can I gift a subject to someone else?
faq.a: Absolutely. You can send Subject 01 — What's Wrong with the Church — to anyone, free, with a personal note from you. Use the 'Gift a subject' tile at the top of this page. It takes 30 seconds and they'll receive it with your name on it.

family.headline: You don’t study here. You belong here.
family.body: Futures Leadership College is embedded inside a living, growing church. That means your professors lead real churches, your classmates become lifelong friends, and the mission you’re being trained for is already underway around you.

personas.headline: Which of these is happening inside you?

items.label: School leaver
items.copy: This next year matters too much for me to wing it.

items.label: Parent
items.copy: My kid only gets one year like this. I want it to actually build them — not just keep them busy.

items.label: Spiritually restless
items.copy: I'm not leaving church. But I'm tired of pretending Sunday is enough.

items.label: Biblically curious
items.copy: I've been reading the Bible for years. I'm starting to suspect I've barely scratched it.

items.label: Sensing a calling
items.copy: I've felt the pull for years. I'm done watching from the sidelines.

items.label: The builder
items.copy: I've spent a decade building. I want what I build next to outlive me.

items.label: Distance learner
items.copy: Geography shouldn't decide whether I get to grow.

items.label: One subject at a time
items.copy: A whole year is more than I can promise right now. But I can say yes to one subject.

facultyIntro.headline: Taught by people who are actually doing it.
facultyIntro.body: Every faculty member leads or has led a local church. You won’t be taught theory by academics — you’ll be shaped by practitioners who have planted, built, and led real communities.
